lines.english: Lesson 2
lines.chinese: 第2课
lines.raw: Lesson 2 | 第2课

lines.english: Thirteen equals one
lines.chinese: 十三等于一
lines.raw: Thirteen equals one | 十三等于一

lines.english: Was the vicar pleased that the clock was striking? Why?
lines.chinese: 牧师对钟声响起感到高兴吗？为什么？
lines.raw: Was the vicar pleased that the clock was striking? Why? | 牧师对钟声响起感到高兴吗？为什么？

lines.english: Our vicar is always raising money for one cause or another,
lines.chinese: 我们的牧师总是为这样或那样的缘故筹款，
lines.raw: Our vicar is always raising money for one cause or another, | 我们的牧师总是为这样或那样的缘故筹款，

lines.english: but he has never managed to get enough money to have the church clock repaired.
lines.chinese: 但他从未筹集到足够的钱来修理教堂的时钟。
lines.raw: but he has never managed to get enough money to have the church clock repaired. | 但他从未筹集到足够的钱来修理教堂的时钟。

lines.english: The big clock which used to strike the hours day and night was damaged many years ago and has been silent ever since.
lines.chinese: 那座日夜报时的大钟多年前损坏了，从那以后就一直沉默不语。
lines.raw: The big clock which used to strike the hours day and night was damaged many years ago and has been silent ever since. | 那座日夜报时的大钟多年前损坏了，从那以后就一直沉默不语。

lines.english: One night, however, our vicar woke up with a start:the clock was striking the hours!
lines.chinese: 然而，一天晚上，我们的牧师突然惊醒：钟正在报时！
lines.raw: One night, however, our vicar woke up with a start:the clock was striking the hours! | 然而，一天晚上，我们的牧师突然惊醒：钟正在报时！

lines.english: Looking at his watch, he saw that it was one o'clock,
lines.chinese: 他看了看表，发现是一点钟，
lines.raw: Looking at his watch, he saw that it was one o'clock, | 他看了看表，发现是一点钟，

lines.english: but the bell struck thirteen times before it stopped.
lines.chinese: 但钟声响了十三次才停下来。
lines.raw: but the bell struck thirteen times before it stopped. | 但钟声响了十三次才停下来。

lines.english: Armed with a torch, the vicar went up into the clock tower to see what was going on.
lines.chinese: 牧师拿着手电筒，爬上钟楼，想看看发生了什么事。
lines.raw: Armed with a torch, the vicar went up into the clock tower to see what was going on. | 牧师拿着手电筒，爬上钟楼，想看看发生了什么事。

lines.english: In the torchlight, he caught sight of a figure whom he immediately recognized as Bill Wilkins, our local grocer.
lines.chinese: 在手电筒的光束中，他看到一个人影，他立刻认出那是比尔·威尔金斯，我们当地的杂货商。
lines.raw: In the torchlight, he caught sight of a figure whom he immediately recognized as Bill Wilkins, our local grocer. | 在手电筒的光束中，他看到一个人影，他立刻认出那是比尔·威尔金斯，我们当地的杂货商。

lines.english: 'Whatever are you doing up here Bill?' asked the vicar in surprise.
lines.chinese: “比尔，你在这上面做什么？”牧师惊讶地问道。
lines.raw: 'Whatever are you doing up here Bill?' asked the vicar in surprise. | “比尔，你在这上面做什么？”牧师惊讶地问道。

lines.english: 'I'm trying to repair the bell, ' answered Bill.
lines.chinese: “我正在努力修理钟，”比尔回答道。
lines.raw: 'I'm trying to repair the bell, ' answered Bill. | “我正在努力修理钟，”比尔回答道。

lines.english: 'I've been coming up here night after night for weeks now.
lines.chinese: “我来这里已经好几个星期了，夜复一夜。
lines.raw: 'I've been coming up here night after night for weeks now. | “我来这里已经好几个星期了，夜复一夜。

lines.english: You see, I was hoping to give you a surprise.'
lines.chinese: 你看，我本来希望能给你一个惊喜。”
lines.raw: You see, I was hoping to give you a surprise.' | 你看，我本来希望能给你一个惊喜。”

lines.english: 'You certainly did give me a surprise!' said the vicar.
lines.chinese: “你确实给了我一个惊喜！”牧师说。
lines.raw: 'You certainly did give me a surprise!' said the vicar. | “你确实给了我一个惊喜！”牧师说。

lines.english: 'You've probably woken up everyone in the village as well.
lines.chinese: “你可能也把村里所有人都吵醒了。
lines.raw: 'You've probably woken up everyone in the village as well. | “你可能也把村里所有人都吵醒了。

lines.english: Still, I'm glad the bell is working again.'
lines.chinese: 不过，我很高兴钟又响了。”
lines.raw: Still, I'm glad the bell is working again.' | 不过，我很高兴钟又响了。”

lines.english: 'That's the trouble, vicar, 'answered Bill. 'It's working all right,
lines.chinese: “问题就在这儿，牧师，”比尔回答道。“它确实在响，
lines.raw: 'That's the trouble, vicar, 'answered Bill. 'It's working all right, | “问题就在这儿，牧师，”比尔回答道。“它确实在响，

lines.english: but I'm afraid that at one o'clock it will strike thirteen times and there's nothing I can do about it.'
lines.chinese: 但我担心一点钟的时候它会响十三次，我对此束手无策。”
lines.raw: but I'm afraid that at one o'clock it will strike thirteen times and there's nothing I can do about it.' | 但我担心一点钟的时候它会响十三次，我对此束手无策。”

lines.english: 'We'll get used to that, Bill, 'said the vicar.
lines.chinese: “我们会习惯的，比尔，”牧师说。
lines.raw: 'We'll get used to that, Bill, 'said the vicar. | “我们会习惯的，比尔，”牧师说。

lines.english: 'Thirteen is not as good as one, but it's better than nothing.
lines.chinese: “十三次不如一次好，但总比没有好。
lines.raw: 'Thirteen is not as good as one, but it's better than nothing. | “十三次不如一次好，但总比没有好。

lines.english: Now let's go downstairs and have a cup of tea.'
lines.chinese: 现在我们下去喝杯茶吧。”
lines.raw: Now let's go downstairs and have a cup of tea.' | 现在我们下去喝杯茶吧。”
